title: aldama obtusifolia
intro: Aldama obtusifolia, also known as the "hairy hawthorn", is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Aldama obtusifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
